question: Based on Table 1.3-1 design life comparison, Table 5.3-3 ART projections, Table 5.3-8 PTS screening, and Ch.05 text on surveillance, is the NuScale 60-year RPV integrity case robust?

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It emphasizes compliance with quality assurance and safety codes, proper environmental controls during shipment, and points to other sections for detailed procedures.

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4. It details whether each regulatory guide is applicable, and if so, whether NuScale’s design fully or partially conforms, with references to supporting sections for further information. Several guides are noted as not applicable, while others are partially or fully met.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the NuScale Power Plant’s major design features and parameters with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, and system components, showing that NuScale uses a smaller, simpler design with fewer external loops and pumps, and has a longer design life. The comparison is summarized in tables for easy reference.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the core, steam generators, and pressurizer, serving as a key barrier to fission product release and supporting various reactor components. The RPV and its materials are selected and fabricated according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel throughout its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that all criteria remain within regulatory limits at end-of-life fluence levels.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the basis for establishing operational pressure and temperature limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB), ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes the methodology used to calculate pressure-temperature limit curves, accounting for vessel embrittlement and providing specific limits for various operational conditions to prevent non-ductile failure of the reactor pressure vessel.

nuscale_ch01_0419.title: DSRS 10.2.3, Rev 0: Turbine Rotor IntegrityII.2 Fracture Toughness
nuscale_ch01_0419.summary: This section states that the NuScale plant design uses physical barriers to protect safety-related structures, systems, and components (SSCs) from turbine missile effects, as specified in regulatory guidance. As a result, requirements related to turbine rotor integrity, including fracture toughness, materials selection, and inspections, are not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0430.title: SRP 10.3.6, Rev 3: Steam and Feedwater System MaterialsII.2 Fracture Toughness of Class 2 and 3 Components
nuscale_ch01_0430.summary: This section states that the NuScale design does not include any Class 2 or 3 components, so the fracture toughness requirements of SRP 10.3.6, Rev 3 are not applicable. The document also summarizes conformance or departures from other regulatory criteria for various steam and feedwater system components, noting specific applicability and compliance for each.

nuscale_ch01_0484.title: DSRS 11.3, Rev 0: Gaseous Waste Management SystemII.10 Relevant RGs, ISG, and BTP
nuscale_ch01_0484.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Gaseous Waste Management System (DSRS 11.3, Rev 0). Most criteria are met, with partial conformance noted for dose design objectives and features minimizing contamination, as some site-specific responsibilities are deferred to the COL applicant. The system otherwise conforms to requirements for design, seismic classification, filtration, automatic controls, and safety features.

nuscale_ch01_0477.title: DSRS 11.3, Rev 0: Gaseous Waste Management SystemII.3 Seismic Design and Quality Group Classification of Components and Structures Housing Gaseous Waste Management System
nuscale_ch01_0477.summary: This section confirms that the seismic design and quality group classification of components and structures housing the Gaseous Waste Management System fully conforms to regulatory requirements, with no exceptions noted. The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report demonstrates compliance with DSRS 11.3, Rev 0, ensuring that these systems are designed to withstand seismic events as required.

edges.description: Section A describes the inspection, handling, and storage requirements for the NuScale RPV, referencing compliance with ASME and NQA-1 standards, while Section B lists the NuScale FSAR's conformance with various regulatory guides, indicating how the design and operational features align with regulatory expectations; thus, Section A provides specific implementation details that support the general regulatory conformance claims in Section B.

Section A describes the inspection, handling, and storage requirements for the NuScale RPV, referencing standards that ensure its integrity, while Section B provides a comparative table of design life and parameters between NuScale and typical PWRs, connecting them through the design life values relevant to assessing RPV integrity robustness.

Section A references Section B by specifying that the nondestructive examinations performed on the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) are described in Section 5.3.1, which contains the material and construction details of the RPV.

Section A describes the inspection, handling, and storage requirements for the RPV to maintain its integrity, while Section B discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, both contributing to the overall assurance of RPV integrity over its design life.

Section A lists regulatory guidance documents and the NuScale FSAR's conformance with them, while Section B provides a comparison of NuScale's design features (including design life) with those of typical PWRs, thus Section B provides context for evaluating whether NuScale's design features (referenced in Section A) are robust relative to industry norms.

Section A lists regulatory guidance and conformance statements for radiation protection design features, while Section B describes the physical design and function of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV); the relationship is that Section A's regulatory criteria provide requirements that Section B's RPV design must address or conform to.

Section A lists regulatory guidance and conformance statements relevant to radiation protection and reactor vessel integrity, while Section B describes the implementation of operating procedures to ensure compliance with technical specifications for reactor vessel integrity, thus Section A provides the regulatory criteria that Section B's procedures are intended to satisfy.

Section A provides a comparative overview of NuScale's design life and parameters relative to typical PWRs, while Section B details the specific materials and configuration of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel (RPV), connecting them through the design life and integrity expectations for the RPV as established in the comparative context.

Section A provides nominal design values for the NuScale reactor vessel in comparison to typical PWRs, which serve as baseline parameters that Section B's operating procedures must respect to ensure vessel integrity and compliance with technical specifications.

Section A describes the physical and functional characteristics of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B outlines the requirement to develop operating procedures that ensure the RPV's pressure-temperature limits, as established by its design and material properties, are not exceeded during operation.

Section A discusses compliance with ASME BPVC requirements for welds and attachments on the RPV, while Section B references the inspection and handling requirements that ensure these ASME standards are met, directly connecting the design and fabrication details in A with the procedural and regulatory compliance in B.

Section B provides nominal design life values for the NuScale RPV and typical PWRs, which Section A uses as a basis for discussing compliance with ASME code requirements for RPV attachments and welds, thereby connecting design life comparisons to regulatory code adherence.

Section A references regulatory criteria for fracture toughness and inservice inspection, while Section B provides specific inspection and handling requirements for the reactor pressure vessel, connecting them through the shared focus on ensuring RPV integrity via compliance with ASME codes and inspection protocols.

Section A states that SRP 10.3.6 fracture toughness requirements for Class 2 and 3 components are not applicable to NuScale because it has no such components, while Section B details the inspection and handling requirements for the NuScale reactor pressure vessel (RPV) per ASME standards, indicating that the regulatory fracture toughness requirements in A do not constrain or apply to the RPV integrity measures described in B.

Section A specifies that certain fracture toughness requirements for Class 2 and 3 components are not applicable to NuScale due to design differences, while Section B provides a comparative table (Table 1.3-1) showing NuScale's design life and parameters relative to typical PWRs, connecting them through the context of design feature applicability and regulatory conformance.

Section A discusses compliance with ASME BPVC requirements for RPV attachments, while Section B states that SRP 10.3.6 fracture toughness criteria for Class 2 and 3 components are not applicable to NuScale because it has no such components, connecting them through the regulatory applicability of material and fracture toughness requirements.

Section A and Section B both address fracture toughness requirements for different plant components, but both conclude that the specific regulatory criteria for turbine rotor and Class 2/3 component fracture toughness are not applicable to the NuScale design due to the absence of relevant components, connecting them through their shared exclusion of applicability.

Section A references the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, which includes Section B's detailed requirements for RPV shipment, installation, and inspection, thereby structurally connecting the general regulatory conformance statements in A to the specific technical practices in B.

Section A and Section B both document the NuScale FSAR's conformance with regulatory guidance, with Section A focusing on waste management systems and Section B on radiation protection design features, and both referencing specific Regulatory Guides and their applicability or conformance status, thus providing parallel evidence of regulatory compliance in different technical areas.

Section A references the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, which is the source of the detailed RPV inspection, shipment, and storage requirements described in Section B, thereby structurally connecting the regulatory conformance discussion to the technical implementation details.

Section A and Section B both document the NuScale FSAR's conformance with DSRS regulatory criteria, with Section A focusing on waste management system structures and Section B on radiation protection design features, and both referencing specific regulatory guides and their applicability or partial conformance.

Section B's Table 1.3-1 provides nominal design life values for NuScale and typical PWRs, which Section A references to demonstrate conformance with regulatory criteria for system design and classification.

Section A references Section B by citing the seismic design and quality group classification requirements for components and structures housing the gaseous waste management system, establishing conformance with regulatory criteria.
